`## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
     files (`todo.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
     a specific file.
   - Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
3. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). And `vc-x1 push`'s `preflight`
   stage re-runs the same cycle, which invites treating push as
   the gate rather than a redundant safety-net.
   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
     Work review on every commit; push's `preflight` is a
     safety-net, not the primary gate. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists — to
     both the documented flow and push's `preflight` stage
     (`push.rs`), with a test. (code)
   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
     the carve-out stops making sense.
   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
     pre-assigned — see Idea #2 on fragile version targets.
4. **vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an intro paragraph.**
   A body whose first line is a bullet (`- file: …`) is a
   Prose-Form violation — bodies must open with an intro
   paragraph, then bullets. Today such a body trips jj's arg
   parser (`jj commit -m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a
   stray flag) and push fails with an opaque error. Hit on
   0.62.0-5.
   - Feature, not a parser bug (reframed): push should
     *validate* the body opens with a non-dash intro line and
     flag its absence with a clear, specific error pointing at
     the offending first line — rather than letting jj emit a
     confusing one, or quietly accepting a bullet-first body.
   - Enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, matching
     the Prose-Form convention; we are not "fixing" the parser
     to accept bullet-first bodies.
   - Workaround until the explicit check lands: prepend a
     non-dash intro sentence to the body.
5. **vc-x1 push: support new cycle protocol shape (N:1 code↔bot).**
   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
   title/body. The new cycle protocol has a different shape on
   each side:
   - code side: fully committed before push (N commits via
     merge), nothing to commit at push time
   - `.claude` side: one commit with its own message (distinct
     from any code commit) and a multi-line `ochid:` listing
     all N code commits

   Teach push to:
   - detect this shape
   - skip `commit-app` when code WC is empty
   - compose a `.claude`-specific message
   - emit multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]]

   Today's workaround: pre-commit `.claude` manually, then
   `vc-x1 push <bm> --from bookmark-both --yes`.
6. **vc-x1 push --squash: symmetric squash on both repos.**
   Automate Option F (manually exercised in 0.59.0
   close-out): app-side squash + bot-side description
   rewrite + force-push, atomically. Without this,
   squash is a manual recipe that future cycles must
   follow each time.
   - App side: squash cycle commits into one new commit;

# Chores-13

Continuation of `chores-12.md` (closed at `0.62.x`). This
file covers the `0.63.0` cycle onward.

Subsection headers that record a release use the
trailing-version format from AGENTS.md: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-13
starts at `[1]`.

## docs: adopt AGENTS.md (0.63.0)

Commits: [[1]]

The bot-instructions file is renamed `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md`
so Zed and the broader agent-tooling ecosystem (which now
default to `AGENTS.md`) read it natively. Claude Code still
auto-loads only `CLAUDE.md`, so a one-line `CLAUDE.md` import
shim (`@AGENTS.md`) keeps it working — both tools read one
canonical file.

- **Single canonical file** — `AGENTS.md` holds the content;
  `CLAUDE.md` is a one-line `@AGENTS.md` import for Claude
  Code's loader. Nothing human-facing points at the shim.
- **Live references repointed** — every `CLAUDE.md` mention
  and `#anchor` link in live docs (`README.md`,
  `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `notes/`, the `src/push.rs` pre-commit
  doc comment, and `AGENTS.md` itself) now names `AGENTS.md`,
  so links resolve in editors and on GitHub.
- **History left as written** — `CLAUDE.md` prose in
  `chores-01..12` and `done.md` records what was true at the
  time and is untouched; only the live navigational anchor
  links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers
  (`#reference-numbering`) were repointed so they don't
  dangle against the one-line `CLAUDE.md` shim.

### Why an import shim, not a symlink

A symlink `CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md` also satisfies Claude
Code's loader, but the `@AGENTS.md` import was chosen
because it is explicit and visible in a diff and does not
depend on symlink semantics (git symlink handling,
cross-platform checkout). Separately, GitHub renders a
symlinked `.md` as a stub page rather than the target's
markdown, so a `CLAUDE.md#anchor` link through a symlink
would not resolve — moot here since live links now point
straight at `AGENTS.md`, but it reinforces keeping
`CLAUDE.md` a real file.

## docs: tighten after-finalize rule (0.63.1)

Commits: [[2]]

The stop-and-wait rule in `cycle-protocol.md` was titled
`### After finalize: stop and wait` and phrased as "final
words go in the approval prompt *before* executing finalize"
— wording that assumes finalize is a separate,
manually-invoked step and names only finalize as the
trigger. With `vc-x1 push` the push and `vc-x1 finalize` on
`.claude` are bundled as the tail stages, so there is no
"before finalize" gap left to speak in once the push is
invoked. After `0.63.0`'s push the bot emitted a closing
summary *after* `vc-x1 push` returned, violating the rule
(and likely riding into the `.claude` commit via the
`--delay 10` window).

- **Renamed to "After push or finalize"** — both triggers
  are named, in the title and the paragraph: the remote
  crossing is the push itself, and `vc-x1 finalize` on
  `.claude` is the detached step. This also covers a manual
  `jj git push` (push, no finalize) and a manual
  `vc-x1 finalize` (no wrapper).
- **Wrapper case spelled out** — `vc-x1 push` does both as
  tail stages (`push-app`, then `vc-x1 finalize` on
  `.claude`): closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper,
  and the bot does not purposely emit anything after it
  returns. The rule names the `vc-x1 finalize` operation, not
  the wrapper's internal `finalize-claude` stage label.

The bot thinks the original lapse was a compliance slip (the
rule was read and quoted, then broken), not a comprehension
gap; the doc change closes the one genuine ambiguity (the
wrapper bundles push + finalize, leaving no gap to speak in)
rather than expecting prose alone to prevent the slip. The
rule lives only in `cycle-protocol.md`; AGENTS.md already
mandates reading it before commit work, so no duplicate
restatement was added there.

## docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe (0.64.0)

Commits: [[3]]

Multi-commit cycles default to the merge-non-ff trapezoid,
but the recipe is split between `cycle-protocol.md` and
AGENTS.md with wrinkles re-derived each time (exercised
manually on `0.62.0`). Codify the full recipe and
standardize the flag spelling. Split out of Idea #1 (the
broader cycle-protocol.md codification).

- Recipe: `jj rebase -r <closeout> --onto <prev-closeout>
  --onto <work-tip>` (first `--onto` = trunk → first
  parent), then `jj new <merge>` to lift `@` above the
  merge, then `jj git push --bookmark main`.
- Standardize on `--onto` / `-o` (canonical), not the
  `-d` alias — update AGENTS.md's `-d` spellings (jj
  Basics + the post-amend `jj new` note) to `--onto`.
- `@` reverts to the work-tip's content until the
  `jj new` (the "post-amend `jj new`" gotcha already in
  AGENTS.md).
- Post-hoc caveat: if the cycle was already pushed
  keep-separate (as `0.62.0` was), the rebase needs
  `--ignore-immutable` and the push is a force-update of
  `main`; the standard recipe assumes the merge is set up
  before the close-out push.

### As-built ladder

- 0.64.0-0 Preparation — version bump, 0.63.1 `Commits:`
  backfill, item pickup. Also clarified the Preparation
  step itself: a Cargo.lock-update bullet, reworded the
  Todo "move into In Progress", and a Prose-form intro
  line at the top of `cycle-protocol.md`.
- 0.64.0-1 cycle-protocol.md — promote the recipe to a
  `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection (rebase → `jj new`
  lift → push, with the parents broken out as sub-bullets
  and a post-hoc caveat); trim the shape bullet to point
  at it. Also reword `### Shape at close-out push`: the
  intro reframed (work done, shape chosen at push,
  post-publish change is a remote rewrite) and Merge
  non-ff tagged as the current default.
- 0.64.0-2 AGENTS.md — standardize `-d` → `--onto` in the
  post-amend note; add a jj Basics bullet for the
  `--onto`/`-o` spelling; sub-bullet the post-amend
  command list; reframe the note around jj's empty-`@`
  final form, dropping the merge-specific example and the
  HEAD-detach mechanism (the recipe now owns the merge
  sequence).
- 0.64.0-3 review round — make `### Merge non-ff recipe`
  step 2 self-contained (own the empty-`@` why inline) and
  drop the AGENTS.md post-amend `jj new` note entirely. The
  note's non-merge cases proved mis-categorized: `jj squash
  --into` leaves `@` clean on top, and `jj edit` lands `@`
  on the commit deliberately (you `jj new` when done, not
  to restore). One-directional ref (Shape → recipe), no
  circle.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Chores-13

Continuation of `chores-12.md` (closed at `0.62.x`). This
file covers the `0.63.0` cycle onward.

Subsection headers that record a release use the
trailing-version format from AGENTS.md: `## Description (X.Y.Z)`.
Reference numbering is file-local — see
[`AGENTS.md`](../../AGENTS.md#reference-numbering); chores-13
starts at `[1]`.

## docs: adopt AGENTS.md (0.63.0)

Commits: [[1]]

The bot-instructions file is renamed `CLAUDE.md` → `AGENTS.md`
so Zed and the broader agent-tooling ecosystem (which now
default to `AGENTS.md`) read it natively. Claude Code still
auto-loads only `CLAUDE.md`, so a one-line `CLAUDE.md` import
shim (`@AGENTS.md`) keeps it working — both tools read one
canonical file.

- **Single canonical file** — `AGENTS.md` holds the content;
  `CLAUDE.md` is a one-line `@AGENTS.md` import for Claude
  Code's loader. Nothing human-facing points at the shim.
- **Live references repointed** — every `CLAUDE.md` mention
  and `#anchor` link in live docs (`README.md`,
  `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `notes/`, the `src/push.rs` pre-commit
  doc comment, and `AGENTS.md` itself) now names `AGENTS.md`,
  so links resolve in editors and on GitHub.
- **History left as written** — `CLAUDE.md` prose in
  `chores-01..12` and `done.md` records what was true at the
  time and is untouched; only the live navigational anchor
  links in the `chores-10/11/12` headers
  (`#reference-numbering`) were repointed so they don't
  dangle against the one-line `CLAUDE.md` shim.

### Why an import shim, not a symlink

A symlink `CLAUDE.md → AGENTS.md` also satisfies Claude
Code's loader, but the `@AGENTS.md` import was chosen
because it is explicit and visible in a diff and does not
depend on symlink semantics (git symlink handling,
cross-platform checkout). Separately, GitHub renders a
symlinked `.md` as a stub page rather than the target's
markdown, so a `CLAUDE.md#anchor` link through a symlink
would not resolve — moot here since live links now point
straight at `AGENTS.md`, but it reinforces keeping
`CLAUDE.md` a real file.

## docs: tighten after-finalize rule (0.63.1)

Commits: [[2]]

The stop-and-wait rule in `cycle-protocol.md` was titled
`### After finalize: stop and wait` and phrased as "final
words go in the approval prompt *before* executing finalize"
— wording that assumes finalize is a separate,
manually-invoked step and names only finalize as the
trigger. With `vc-x1 push` the push and `vc-x1 finalize` on
`.claude` are bundled as the tail stages, so there is no
"before finalize" gap left to speak in once the push is
invoked. After `0.63.0`'s push the bot emitted a closing
summary *after* `vc-x1 push` returned, violating the rule
(and likely riding into the `.claude` commit via the
`--delay 10` window).

- **Renamed to "After push or finalize"** — both triggers
  are named, in the title and the paragraph: the remote
  crossing is the push itself, and `vc-x1 finalize` on
  `.claude` is the detached step. This also covers a manual
  `jj git push` (push, no finalize) and a manual
  `vc-x1 finalize` (no wrapper).
- **Wrapper case spelled out** — `vc-x1 push` does both as
  tail stages (`push-app`, then `vc-x1 finalize` on
  `.claude`): closing words go *before* invoking the wrapper,
  and the bot does not purposely emit anything after it
  returns. The rule names the `vc-x1 finalize` operation, not
  the wrapper's internal `finalize-claude` stage label.

The bot thinks the original lapse was a compliance slip (the
rule was read and quoted, then broken), not a comprehension
gap; the doc change closes the one genuine ambiguity (the
wrapper bundles push + finalize, leaving no gap to speak in)
rather than expecting prose alone to prevent the slip. The
rule lives only in `cycle-protocol.md`; AGENTS.md already
mandates reading it before commit work, so no duplicate
restatement was added there.

## docs: codify merge-non-ff recipe (0.64.0)

Commits: [[3]]

Multi-commit cycles default to the merge-non-ff trapezoid,
but the recipe is split between `cycle-protocol.md` and
AGENTS.md with wrinkles re-derived each time (exercised
manually on `0.62.0`). Codify the full recipe and
standardize the flag spelling. Split out of Idea #1 (the
broader cycle-protocol.md codification).

- Recipe: `jj rebase -r <closeout> --onto <prev-closeout>
  --onto <work-tip>` (first `--onto` = trunk → first
  parent), then `jj new <merge>` to lift `@` above the
  merge, then `jj git push --bookmark main`.
- Standardize on `--onto` / `-o` (canonical), not the
  `-d` alias — update AGENTS.md's `-d` spellings (jj
  Basics + the post-amend `jj new` note) to `--onto`.
- `@` reverts to the work-tip's content until the
  `jj new` (the "post-amend `jj new`" gotcha already in
  AGENTS.md).
- Post-hoc caveat: if the cycle was already pushed
  keep-separate (as `0.62.0` was), the rebase needs
  `--ignore-immutable` and the push is a force-update of
  `main`; the standard recipe assumes the merge is set up
  before the close-out push.

### As-built ladder

- 0.64.0-0 Preparation — version bump, 0.63.1 `Commits:`
  backfill, item pickup. Also clarified the Preparation
  step itself: a Cargo.lock-update bullet, reworded the
  Todo "move into In Progress", and a Prose-form intro
  line at the top of `cycle-protocol.md`.
- 0.64.0-1 cycle-protocol.md — promote the recipe to a
  `### Merge non-ff recipe` subsection (rebase → `jj new`
  lift → push, with the parents broken out as sub-bullets
  and a post-hoc caveat); trim the shape bullet to point
  at it. Also reword `### Shape at close-out push`: the
  intro reframed (work done, shape chosen at push,
  post-publish change is a remote rewrite) and Merge
  non-ff tagged as the current default.
- 0.64.0-2 AGENTS.md — standardize `-d` → `--onto` in the
  post-amend note; add a jj Basics bullet for the
  `--onto`/`-o` spelling; sub-bullet the post-amend
  command list; reframe the note around jj's empty-`@`
  final form, dropping the merge-specific example and the
  HEAD-detach mechanism (the recipe now owns the merge
  sequence).
- 0.64.0-3 review round — make `### Merge non-ff recipe`
  step 2 self-contained (own the empty-`@` why inline) and
  drop the AGENTS.md post-amend `jj new` note entirely. The
  note's non-merge cases proved mis-categorized: `jj squash
  --into` leaves `@` clean on top, and `jj edit` lands `@`
  on the commit deliberately (you `jj new` when done, not
  to restore). One-directional ref (Shape → recipe), no
  circle.

## docs: record finalize ochid-loss bug (0.65.1)

Commits: [[4]]

The fc project hit a `finalize` squash that silently dropped a
described journal's message and its 6 `ochid:` trailers
(2026-06-08; recovered 2026-06-10 via op-log surgery). The bug
write-up and two doc-link fixes sat uncommitted in the working
copy; this cycle lands them, ports fc's AGENTS.md additions
back, and queues the fix as Todo #1.

- bugs.md gains the incident as Bugs #1: the failure mode
  (finalize's `--use-destination-message` assumption inverts
  when the journal is described on `@`), the cost (dangling
  code-side ochids), and the fix direction (detect source-only
  `ochid:` trailers; refuse or merge instead of dropping).
- fc additions ported back:
  - AGENTS.md: use-jj-not-git (jj Basics intro) and
    one-command-per-shell-invocation (Working Directory).
  - cycle-protocol.md `## ochid trailers`: path semantics,
    trailer-format example, `### Resolvability`,
    `### .vc-config.toml`.
  - cycle-protocol.md `### vc-x1 push wrapper`: push injects
    the `ochid:` trailers itself — don't hand-write them.
- Stale "active chores file" prose genericized: notes/README.md
  no longer names chores-10 as the active file (the
  refactor-tracking tables remain in chores-10.md, reachable
  via ARCHITECTURE.md); ARCHITECTURE.md's further-reading
  bullets simplified to match.

### As-built ladder

The planned `-0`/`-1`/close-out ladder collapsed to a single
commit once `vc-x1 push` (one app commit per invocation, ochid
trailers injected by the tool) became the commit vehicle:

- 0.65.1 — bug write-up, link fixes, fc port, Todo #1,
  close-out bookkeeping; one `vc-x1 push main`.

input.new_string:
**fix finalize ochid-dropping squash.** `finalize_exec`
(`src/finalize.rs`) squashes with `--use-destination-message`
unconditionally, so a described journal on `@` loses its message
and `ochid:` trailers — this broke the code↔session cross-links
in the fc project ([bugs.md](bugs.md) Bugs #1). Decision: refuse
the squash when the source message carries `ochid:` trailers the
destination's message lacks — the destination has its own ochids,
so any automatic message merge guesses wrong in some direction.

   - 0.65.2-0 Preparation: backfill 0.65.1 Commits ref, bump
     version, pick up this entry, open chores section (current)
   - 0.65.2-1 ochid-trailer guard in finalize: extract + compare
     trailers, refuse in preflight and finalize_exec; unit tests
   - 0.65.2 close-out and validation

39	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.

42	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
43	 number. Long-tail entries
44	 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
45	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper

49	1. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
50	   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
51	   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
52	   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
53	   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
54	   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
55	   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
56	   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
57	     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
58	     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
59	   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
60	     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
61	     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
62	     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
63	     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
64	   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
65	     files (`todo.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
66	     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
67	     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
68	     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
69	     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
70	     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
71	     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
72	   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
73	     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
74	     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
75	     a specific file.
76	   - Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
77	     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
78	2. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
79	   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
80	   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
81	   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
82	   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). And `vc-x1 push`'s `preflight`
83	   stage re-runs the same cycle, which invites treating push as
84	   the gate rather than a redundant safety-net.
85	   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
86	     Work review on every commit; push's `preflight` is a
87	     safety-net, not the primary gate. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
88	     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
89	   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
90	     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
91	     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists — to
92	     both the documented flow and push's `preflight` stage
93	     (`push.rs`), with a test. (code)
94	   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
95	     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
96	     the carve-out stops making sense.
97	   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
98	     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
99	     pre-assigned — see Idea #2 on fragile version targets.
100	3. **vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an intro paragraph.**
101	   A body whose first line is a bullet (`- file: …`) is a
102	   Prose-Form violation — bodies must open with an intro
103	   paragraph, then bullets. Today such a body trips jj's arg
104	   parser (`jj commit -m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a
105	   stray flag) and push fails with an opaque error. Hit on
106	   0.62.0-5.
107	   - Feature, not a parser bug (reframed): push should
108	     *validate* the body opens with a non-dash intro line and
109	     flag its absence with a clear, specific error pointing at
110	     the offending first line — rather than letting jj emit a
111	     confusing one, or quietly accepting a bullet-first body.
112	   - Enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, matching
113	     the Prose-Form convention; we are not "fixing" the parser
114	     to accept bullet-first bodies.
115	   - Workaround until the explicit check lands: prepend a
116	     non-dash intro sentence to the body.
117	4. **vc-x1 push: support new cycle protocol shape (N:1 code↔bot).**
118	   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
119	   title/body. The new cycle protocol has a different shape on
120	   each side:
121	   - code side: fully committed before push (N commits via
122	     merge), nothing to commit at push time
123	   - `.claude` side: one commit with its own message (distinct
124	     from any code commit) and a multi-line `ochid:` listing
125	     all N code commits
126	
127	   Teach push to:
128	   - detect this shape
129	   - skip `commit-app` when code WC is empty
130	   - compose a `.claude`-specific message
131	   - emit multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]]
132	
133	   Today's workaround: pre-commit `.claude` manually, then
134	   `vc-x1 push <bm> --from bookmark-both --yes`.
135	5. **vc-x1 push --squash: symmetric squash on both repos.**
136	   Automate Option F (manually exercised in 0.59.0
137	   close-out): app-side squash + bot-side description
138	   rewrite + force-push, atomically. Without this,
139	   squash is a manual recipe that future cycles must
140	   follow each time.
141	   - App side: squash cycle commits into one new commit;
142	     capture the squashed chid.
143	   - Bot side: rewrite the prior push commit's
144	     description — replace its per-commit `ochid:`
145	     trailers with one pointing at the squashed chid;
146	     add a rewrite-note acknowledging the change
147	     (preserves historical truth for future readers).
148	   - Force-push bot `main` (rewrites the published
149	     commit; chid preserved via `jj describe`).
150	   - Push app `main`. The new bot commit paired with
151	     this push receives `ochid: /<squashed-chid>` as
152	     normal — produces a 2:1 bot→code pattern that's
153	     already in the protocol's design space.
154	   - Gates `Squash to one commit` as a routine
155	     close-out shape vs. the current manual recipe.
156	6. **single-field `options_flags` leaves → `value` field.**

content.text:
Here's the proposed commit description for the Preparation commit.

**Title:**

```
chore: open ochid-guard cycle (0.65.2-0)
```

**Body:**

```
Open the 0.65.2 cycle: fix finalize's ochid-dropping squash by
refusing the squash when the source message carries ochid:
trailers the destination's message lacks (Bugs #1). Refuse was
chosen over merge — the destination has its own ochids, so any
automatic message merge guesses wrong in some direction.

- notes/chores/chores-13.md: backfill 0.65.1 Commits ref [4]
  (post-rewrite SHA 28a0211a364a); open the 0.65.2 section
  header
- notes/todo.md: Todo #1 picked up into ## In Progress
  (problem statement + refuse decision + ladder); remaining
  entries renumbered 1..8
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.65.1 -> 0.65.2-0
```

May I commit? And do you want this pushed now as an interim push (via `vc-x1 push main`, which pairs it with a `.claude` commit and injects the ochid trailers), or committed locally with `jj commit` and pushed at close-out?

200	   squash + Option F (manual bot-side rewrite). The
201	   rules were exercised manually for the close-out;
202	   this Idea formally codifies them.
203	   - Codify the **ochid invariant** in `## ochid
204	     trailers`: every public ochid must resolve in the
205	     public graph.
206	   - Codify bot-repo rules: never squashed; descriptions
207	     editable (`jj describe` preserves chid).
208	   - Codify squash gating: until `vc-x1 push --squash`
209	     exists, manual symmetric squash (Option F: app
210	     squash + bot-side trailer rewrite + force-push) is
211	     the standard recipe; merge non-ff is the default
212	     shape for multi-commit cycles (the concrete recipe is
213	     its own Todo — "merge-non-ff recipe").
214	   - Sketch cross-repo migration: ochids change at
215	     every merge until the change reaches the canonical
216	     repo's `main`.
217	   - Apply Ideas-aware Preparation/Close-out updates:
218	     Preparation triages `## Ideas` (promote / fold /
219	     drop) before declaring the plan; Close-out
220	     captures unresolved follow-ups into `## Ideas`.
221	
222	2. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
223	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
224	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
225	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
226	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
227	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
228	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
229	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
230	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
231	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
232	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
233	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
234	   workflow.
235	   - No fix yet — capture the problem; triage at a later
236	     Preparation.
237	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
238	     not a pre-assigned version?
239	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
240	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
241	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
242	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
243	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
244	
245	3. **`vc` as a code+conversation provenance tool (grander
246	   ambition).** Today `vc-x1` manages a dual repo (code +
247	   `.claude`) cross-linked by `ochid:`. The larger aim is
248	   to *surface* that link — view history with the
249	   conversation and the code side by side: provenance, the
250	   *why* of a change, not just the *what*. The dual-repo +
251	   `ochid` design is already the substrate; the cross-links
252	   make code↔conversation navigable, so the viewer is UI
253	   over an already-solved data link.
254	   - Build direction: keep resolution/assembly in `vc` — an
255	     editor-agnostic Rust engine/lib extending the
256	     `show` / `chid` / `desc` family ("given a commit,
257	     resolve its ochid and assemble the paired diff +
258	     conversation slice"); the editor add-on is a thin
259	     presentation layer over it.

file.content:
squash + Option F (manual bot-side rewrite). The
   rules were exercised manually for the close-out;
   this Idea formally codifies them.
   - Codify the **ochid invariant** in `## ochid
     trailers`: every public ochid must resolve in the
     public graph.
   - Codify bot-repo rules: never squashed; descriptions
     editable (`jj describe` preserves chid).
   - Codify squash gating: until `vc-x1 push --squash`
     exists, manual symmetric squash (Option F: app
     squash + bot-side trailer rewrite + force-push) is
     the standard recipe; merge non-ff is the default
     shape for multi-commit cycles (the concrete recipe is
     its own Todo — "merge-non-ff recipe").
   - Sketch cross-repo migration: ochids change at
     every merge until the change reaches the canonical
     repo's `main`.
   - Apply Ideas-aware Preparation/Close-out updates:
     Preparation triages `## Ideas` (promote / fold /
     drop) before declaring the plan; Close-out
     captures unresolved follow-ups into `## Ideas`.

2. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
   workflow.
   - No fix yet — capture the problem; triage at a later
     Preparation.
   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
     not a pre-assigned version?
     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
       A contributors email address would do it, but also
       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.

3. **`vc` as a code+conversation provenance tool (grander
   ambition).** Today `vc-x1` manages a dual repo (code +
   `.claude`) cross-linked by `ochid:`. The larger aim is
   to *surface* that link — view history with the
   conversation and the code side by side: provenance, the
   *why* of a change, not just the *what*. The dual-repo +
   `ochid` design is already the substrate; the cross-links
   make code↔conversation navigable, so the viewer is UI
   over an already-solved data link.
   - Build direction: keep resolution/assembly in `vc` — an
     editor-agnostic Rust engine/lib extending the
     `show` / `chid` / `desc` family ("given a commit,
     resolve its ochid and assemble the paired diff +
     conversation slice"); the editor add-on is a thin
     presentation layer over it.

content.text:
Checked — the protocol has **not** changed; the concern was captured as an Idea but never adopted:

- `notes/cycle-protocol.md:206-217` still specifies the title shape `<type>: <short description> (<version>)` with "≤50 chars total (version suffix counts)", and `AGENTS.md:336` still requires commit-recording chores headers / Done entries to use that same versioned string.
- What you're remembering is **Idea #2** in `notes/todo.md:222` — "Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the change lands." It records exactly this instability (the landing version is only fixed at merge, so pre-assigned versions can't be trusted), but it explicitly says "**No fix yet — capture the problem; triage at a later Preparation**", with the open question (what identifies a cycle's commits if not a version?) unresolved.
- The only adopted carve-outs are narrower: sub-cycle ladder *working* titles omit the suffix until close-out collects them (`cycle-protocol.md:569`), and new Todo entries don't pre-assign versions (Todo #2 cites Idea #2 for that).

So as written, the convention still wants `(0.65.2-0)` in the title. Two ways to go:

1. **Keep the suffix for this cycle** — follow the protocol as documented; Idea #2 stays queued for a future triage where the replacement scheme gets designed properly.
2. **Triage Idea #2 now** — this is a Preparation, which is the designated beat for Ideas triage; but dropping the version means deciding the open identifier question and rewriting cycle-protocol.md + AGENTS.md, which is a real scope addition to a bug-fix cycle.

I'd keep the suffix for 0.65.2 and leave Idea #2 for its own cycle. Want me to proceed with the title as proposed, or adjust?

attachment.snippet:
1	# Todo
2	
3	This file contains near term tasks with a short description
4	and reference links to more details.
5	
6	Intro paragraphs in `## Todo` and `## Bugs` should begin every
7	line with 1 leading space so they don't match the `^\d+\. `
8	pattern that locates numbered entries; 2 or 3 spaces also work.
9	
10	## In Progress
11	
12	When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
13	problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
14	by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
15	   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
16	   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
17	   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
18	   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation
19	
20	**fix finalize ochid-dropping squash.** `finalize_exec`
21	(`src/finalize.rs`) squashes with `--use-destination-message`
22	unconditionally, so a described journal on `@` loses its message
23	and `ochid:` trailers — this broke the code↔session cross-links
24	in the fc project ([bugs.md](bugs.md) Bugs #1). Decision: refuse
25	the squash when the source message carries `ochid:` trailers the
26	destination's message lacks — the destination has its own ochids,
27	so any automatic message merge guesses wrong in some direction.
28	
29	   - 0.65.2-0 Preparation: backfill 0.65.1 Commits ref, bump
30	     version, pick up this entry, open chores section (done)
31	   - 0.65.2-1 ochid-trailer guard in finalize: extract + compare
32	     trailers, refuse in preflight and finalize_exec; unit tests
33	   - 0.65.2 close-out and validation
34	
35	## Todo
36	
37	 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
38	 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
39	 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/todo.md` to renumber.
40	 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
41	 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
42	 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
43	 number. Long-tail entries
44	 live in [todo-backlog.md](todo-backlog.md). Use the
45	 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](/AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
46	 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
47	 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).
48	
49	1. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
50	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
51	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
52	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
53	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
54	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
55	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
56	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
57	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
58	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
59	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
60	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
61	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
62	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
63	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
64	     not a pre-assigned version?
65	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
66	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
67	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
68	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
69	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
70	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
71	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
72	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
73	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
74	2. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
75	   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
76	   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
77	   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
78	   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
79	   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
80	   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
81	   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
82	     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
83	     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
84	   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
85	     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
86	     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
87	     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
88	     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
89	   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
90	     files (`todo.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
91	     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
92	     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
93	     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
94	     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
95	     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
96	     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
97	   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
98	     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
99	     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
100	     a specific file.
101	   - Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
102	     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
103	3. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
104	   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
105	   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
106	   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
107	   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). And `vc-x1 push`'s `preflight`
108	   stage re-runs the same cycle, which invites treating push as
109	   the gate rather than a redundant safety-net.
110	   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
111	     Work review on every commit; push's `preflight` is a
112	     safety-net, not the primary gate. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
113	     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
114	   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
115	     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
116	     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists — to
117	     both the documented flow and push's `preflight` stage
118	     (`push.rs`), with a test. (code)
119	   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
120	     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
121	     the carve-out stops making sense.
122	   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
123	     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
124	     pre-assigned — see the Todo "Version-number protocol is
125	     fragile" on fragile version targets.
126	4. **vc-x1 push: validate body opens with an intro paragraph.**
127	   A body whose first line is a bullet (`- file: …`) is a
128	   Prose-Form violation — bodies must open with an intro
129	   paragraph, then bullets. Today such a body trips jj's arg
130	   parser (`jj commit -m "<body>"` reads the leading `-` as a
131	   stray flag) and push fails with an opaque error. Hit on
132	   0.62.0-5.
133	   - Feature, not a parser bug (reframed): push should
134	     *validate* the body opens with a non-dash intro line and
135	     flag its absence with a clear, specific error pointing at
136	     the offending first line — rather than letting jj emit a
137	     confusing one, or quietly accepting a bullet-first body.
138	   - Enforcing the intro is the intended behavior, matching
139	     the Prose-Form convention; we are not "fixing" the parser
140	     to accept bullet-first bodies.
141	   - Workaround until the explicit check lands: prepend a
142	     non-dash intro sentence to the body.
143	5. **vc-x1 push: support new cycle protocol shape (N:1 code↔bot).**
144	   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
145	   title/body. The new cycle protocol has a different shape on
146	   each side:
147	   - code side: fully committed before push (N commits via
148	     merge), nothing to commit at push time
149	   - `.claude` side: one commit with its own message (distinct
150	     from any code commit) and a multi-line `ochid:` listing
151	     all N code commits
152

content.text: Committing via `vc-x1 push main` (the commit vehicle — injects the ochid trailers and pairs the `.claude` commit). Checking the push flags first:

input.description: Show push wrapper flags

content.content:
vc-x1 0.65.1

Dual-repo commit+push+finalize in one resumable command.

Collapses today's manual Commit-Push-Finalize Flow into a
single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a
state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can
resume without re-doing completed stages.

Stages: preflight (fmt/clippy/test) → review (approve diff)
→ message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →
commit-app → commit-claude (skipped if clean) → bookmark-both
→ push-app → finalize-claude. Failures in commit-app /
commit-claude / bookmark-both roll both repos back via
`jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-app.
After push-app succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and
recovery is forward-only.

Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes
to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body
across resumes so only the first invocation needs them.

Usage: vc-x1 push [OPTIONS] [BOOKMARK]

Arguments:
  [BOOKMARK]
          Bookmark to advance in both repos (positional form of `--bookmark`).
          
          Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
          without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an alias
          for scripts and for composition with other args. The two forms
          conflict if both supplied.

Options:
      --bookmark <BOOKMARK>
          Bookmark to advance in both repos (flag form; see positional)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --restart
          Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --from <STAGE>
          Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use)

          Possible values:
          - preflight:       Run fmt / clippy / test / install / retest
          - review:          Present diff for the first approval gate
          - message:         Compose / edit the commit message; present for
            second gate
          - commit-app:      Commit the app repo
          - commit-claude:   Commit the `.claude` session repo (skipped if
            empty)
          - bookmark-both:   Advance both bookmarks to `@-`
          - push-app:        `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`
          - finalize-claude: `vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --squash --push <b>
            ...`

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --step
          Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate

      --status
          Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit

      --recheck
          Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run
          succeeded)

      --no-finalize
          Stop before `finalize-claude` so it can be run manually

      --dry-run
          Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects

      --title <STR>
          Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

      --body <STR>
          Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

  -y, --yes
          Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
          confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both supplied
          in non-interactive contexts

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

content.text:
The 0.65.2-1 work is complete. Ready to commit — please review.

What's in the working copy (`src/finalize.rs` plus the bookkeeping files):

- **Three new functions** in `src/finalize.rs`:
  - `extract_ochids` — pulls the values of column-0 `ochid:` trailer lines from a description (indented mentions don't count; a bare valueless `ochid:` is ignored).
  - `ochids_at_risk` — the pure comparison: source trailers absent from the destination's message, i.e. exactly what `--use-destination-message` would drop.
  - `check_squash_keeps_ochids` — reads both messages via `jj log -T description` and errors with the at-risk list and recovery guidance (`jj describe` the target to merge by hand, or clear the source's description).
- **Two wire-in points**: `preflight` (visible early failure before detach) and `finalize_exec` immediately after the delay sleep — the authoritative check, since a `jj describe` can land between preflight and the delayed squash. In the detached child the error propagates to the existing failure-marker path.
- **Five unit tests** covering extraction (none / trailers / column-0-and-trim edge cases) and the at-risk comparison (source-only trailer detected; undescribed source, identical messages, and trailer-less described source all safe).
- **Live verification** in a scratch jj repo: the exact Bugs #1 scenario (described `@` with an ochid, destination without) is refused with exit 1 and the actionable message; clearing the description lets the normal squash proceed (verified, scratch repo removed).

Cargo cycle: fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, 391 tests pass, `cargo install --locked` done (`vc-x1` now reports 0.65.2-1). Version + lockfile bumped to `0.65.2-1`.

content.content:
636	### Testing push + finalize
637	
638	Always test against a throwaway fixture, never the live workspace.
639	Scaffold one with `vc-x1 init --repo-local <PARENT>` (no GitHub, no
640	network), then run the complete push + finalize flow end-to-end.
641	The code repo uses plain `jj git push`; the session repo uses
642	`vc-x1 finalize` to squash trailing writes and push in one shot.
643	
644	`--repo-local` lays out:
645	```
646	<PARENT>/
647	  remote-code.git/     bare git remote for code repo
648	  remote-claude.git/   bare git remote for .claude session repo
649	  <NAME>/              code repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
650	    .vc-config.toml    path="/",       other-repo=".claude"
651	    .gitignore         /.claude /.git /.jj /target /.vc-x1
652	    .claude/           session repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
653	      .vc-config.toml  path="/.claude", other-repo=".."
654	      .gitignore       .git .jj
655	```
656	
657	```bash
658	parent=$(mktemp -u /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
659	vc-x1 init work --repo-local "$parent"
660	work="$parent/work"
661	session="$parent/work/.claude"
662	
663	# 1. code repo: described commit → advance main → push
664	echo hello > "$work/hello.txt"
665	jj describe @ -R "$work" -m 'feat: add hello.txt'
666	jj bookmark set main -r @ -R "$work"
667	jj git push -R "$work"
668	
669	# 2. session repo: trailing writes → finalize (squash into @-, push)
670	echo notes > "$session/notes.md"
671	vc-x1 finalize --repo "$session" --squash --push main --detach \
672	    --log "$session/finalize.log"
673	
674	# 3. inspect the detached child's log once it's done (≈10s by default)
675	sleep 12 && cat "$session/finalize.log"
676	
677	# 4. cleanup when done
678	rm -rf "$parent"
679	```
680	
681	**Why `jj git push` for code but `finalize` for `.claude`?** The
682	code repo's workflow is a plain dev commit on `@-` that we push
683	directly. The session repo mirrors the bot's runtime pattern:
684	session writes land in `@` (above the last committed dev commit),
685	and `finalize --squash @,@-` folds those trailing writes into the
686	dev commit just before pushing, so one atomic state goes upstream.
687	
688	The log file shows timestamped (nanoseconds) entries with PIDs,
689	covering the full flow: `main` entry/exit, `finalize` entry/exit,
690	`detach` spawn, and `finalize_exec` in the child process.
691	
692	Example — detached finalize against a fresh fixture. What the user
693	sees in the terminal (the parent process) is the pre-flight plan and
694	the detach confirmation; the child's work continues in the background:
695	```
696	$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "$session" --squash --push main \
697	    --detach --delay 1 --log "$session/finalize.log"
698	finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work/.claude
699	finalize: set bookmark 'main' mwutluxv 31a49010 → mwutluxv 31a49010 (@-)
700	finalize: push 'main' to remote
701	finalize: detached (pid 103787), log: /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/finalize.log
702	```
703	
704	A few seconds later the log file (authoritative when the caller
705	closes the child's pipes) shows the full run, including the child's
706	own squash/push output:
707	```
708	$ cat "$session/finalize.log"
709	[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work/.claude
710	[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: set bookmark 'main' mwutluxv 31a49010 → mwutluxv 31a49010 (@-)
711	[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: push 'main' to remote
712	[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: detached (pid 103787), log: /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/finalize.log
713	[INFO ] vc_x1::common: Working copy  (@) now at: ovumtpup fa1cb861 (empty) (no description set)
714	Parent commit (@-)      : mwutluxv 584571ba main* | initial commit
715	[INFO ] vc_x1::common: Nothing changed.
716	[INFO ] vc_x1::common: Changes to push to origin:
717	  Move sideways bookmark main from 31a490102db7 to 584571ba54af
718	```
719	
720	Pre-flight failures (bookmark missing, non-tracking remote, squash
721	revset unresolved, push target lacks a description) exit the parent
722	synchronously with a non-zero status and a pointed error on stderr,
723	before the child is ever spawned.
724	
725	## Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers

file.content:
### Testing push + finalize

Always test against a throwaway fixture, never the live workspace.
Scaffold one with `vc-x1 init --repo-local <PARENT>` (no GitHub, no
network), then run the complete push + finalize flow end-to-end.
The code repo uses plain `jj git push`; the session repo uses
`vc-x1 finalize` to squash trailing writes and push in one shot.

`--repo-local` lays out:
```
<PARENT>/
  remote-code.git/     bare git remote for code repo
  remote-claude.git/   bare git remote for .claude session repo
  <NAME>/              code repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
    .vc-config.toml    path="/",       other-repo=".claude"
    .gitignore         /.claude /.git /.jj /target /.vc-x1
    .claude/           session repo (jj colocated, main tracks origin)
      .vc-config.toml  path="/.claude", other-repo=".."
      .gitignore       .git .jj
```

```bash
parent=$(mktemp -u /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
vc-x1 init work --repo-local "$parent"
work="$parent/work"
session="$parent/work/.claude"

# 1. code repo: described commit → advance main → push
echo hello > "$work/hello.txt"
jj describe @ -R "$work" -m 'feat: add hello.txt'
jj bookmark set main -r @ -R "$work"
jj git push -R "$work"

# 2. session repo: trailing writes → finalize (squash into @-, push)
echo notes > "$session/notes.md"
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$session" --squash --push main --detach \
    --log "$session/finalize.log"

# 3. inspect the detached child's log once it's done (≈10s by default)
sleep 12 && cat "$session/finalize.log"

# 4. cleanup when done
rm -rf "$parent"
```

**Why `jj git push` for code but `finalize` for `.claude`?** The
code repo's workflow is a plain dev commit on `@-` that we push
directly. The session repo mirrors the bot's runtime pattern:
session writes land in `@` (above the last committed dev commit),
and `finalize --squash @,@-` folds those trailing writes into the
dev commit just before pushing, so one atomic state goes upstream.

The log file shows timestamped (nanoseconds) entries with PIDs,
covering the full flow: `main` entry/exit, `finalize` entry/exit,
`detach` spawn, and `finalize_exec` in the child process.

Example — detached finalize against a fresh fixture. What the user
sees in the terminal (the parent process) is the pre-flight plan and
the detach confirmation; the child's work continues in the background:
```
$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "$session" --squash --push main \
    --detach --delay 1 --log "$session/finalize.log"
finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work/.claude
finalize: set bookmark 'main' mwutluxv 31a49010 → mwutluxv 31a49010 (@-)
finalize: push 'main' to remote
finalize: detached (pid 103787), log: /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/finalize.log
```

A few seconds later the log file (authoritative when the caller
closes the child's pipes) shows the full run, including the child's
own squash/push output:
```
$ cat "$session/finalize.log"
[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/work/.claude
[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: set bookmark 'main' mwutluxv 31a49010 → mwutluxv 31a49010 (@-)
[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: push 'main' to remote
[INFO ] vc_x1::finalize: finalize: detached (pid 103787), log: /tmp/vc-x1-test-8PD4x8/finalize.log
[INFO ] vc_x1::common: Working copy  (@) now at: ovumtpup fa1cb861 (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : mwutluxv 584571ba main* | initial commit
[INFO ] vc_x1::common: Nothing changed.
[INFO ] vc_x1::common: Changes to push to origin:
  Move sideways bookmark main from 31a490102db7 to 584571ba54af
```

Pre-flight failures (bookmark missing, non-tracking remote, squash
revset unresolved, push target lacks a description) exit the parent
synchronously with a non-zero status and a pointed error on stderr,
before the child is ever spawned.

## Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers

content.text:
Very feasible — the README already has exactly this pattern: `### Testing push + finalize` (README.md:636) walks a throwaway `/tmp` fixture through the real flow with numbered steps and a cleanup line. A sibling subsection slots right in, and the guard is actually *easier* to demo than push+finalize because it fires before any push — a plain `jj git init` scratch repo works, no `--repo-local` remotes, fully offline.

I'd cover three cases, the third being the interesting one:

1. **Synchronous refusal** — committed "prev journal", then describe `@` with an `ochid:` trailer, run `vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0` → exit 1 naming the at-risk trailer (this is the Bugs #1 scenario; I ran exactly this in `/tmp` during verification).
2. **Normal pass** — clear `@`'s description, rerun → squash proceeds.
3. **Detached race → failure marker** — the case unit tests can't reach cheaply, and the real incident path: launch `finalize --squash --delay 5 --detach` with a clean `@` (parent preflight passes), then `jj describe` the ochid trailer onto `@` *inside the delay window*; the child's post-delay re-check refuses, writes the failure marker, and the next `vc-x1` invocation surfaces the `warn: previous finalize failure` block. That demonstrates both the exec-time guard and the marker plumbing in one short sequence — the 5-second window is generous enough to be reliable by hand.

One scope note: we're mid-review on 0.65.2-1, so the natural move is to fold this README section into the open `-1` commit (it documents the capability `-1` adds) rather than grow the ladder. Alternative is a separate `-2` docs rung if you prefer the code and docs split.

Want me to write the section into README.md as part of `-1`?

input.new_string:
Pre-flight failures (bookmark missing, non-tracking remote, squash
revset unresolved, push target lacks a description) exit the parent
synchronously with a non-zero status and a pointed error on stderr,
before the child is ever spawned.

### Testing the ochid-trailer guard

`finalize --squash` refuses to drop `ochid:` trailers: when the
squash source's message carries trailers the destination's message
lacks, `--use-destination-message` would silently discard them and
leave the counterpart repo's cross-links dangling (the incident is
recorded as Bugs #1 in [notes/bugs.md](notes/bugs.md)). The guard
runs twice — in the parent's pre-flight and again in the child
right before the squash — so it also catches a `jj describe` that
lands during the `--delay` window.

The guard fires before any push, so no remote is needed; a plain
scratch repo suffices:

```bash
repo=$(mktemp -u /tmp/vc-x1-guard-XXXXXX)
jj git init "$repo"

# previous journal: committed, no trailer
echo a > "$repo/file.txt"
jj commit -m 'prev journal' -R "$repo"

# 1. bug scenario: journal described on @ WITH an ochid trailer
echo b >> "$repo/file.txt"
jj describe -R "$repo" -m 'new journal

ochid: /abc123abc123'

# refused synchronously: exit 1, the error names the trailer
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0

# 2. normal case: clear the description; the squash proceeds
jj describe -R "$repo" -m ''
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0

# 3. detached race: a clean @ passes the parent's pre-flight,
#    then a describe lands inside the delay window — the child's
#    re-check refuses and drops a failure marker
echo c >> "$repo/file.txt"
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 5 --detach
jj describe -R "$repo" -m 'late journal

ochid: /def456def456'

# after the child exits (~5s), retrying surfaces the marker
# (any vc-x1 command does) and then refuses synchronously
sleep 6
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0

# cleanup
rm -rf "$repo"
```

Step 1 and the step-3 retry both exit 1 with:

```
error: refusing squash @ → @-: the source message has ochid:
trailers the destination's message lacks (/abc123abc123) —
--use-destination-message would drop them; merge the messages by
hand (`jj describe @- -R <repo>`) or clear the source's
description, then retry
```

and the step-3 retry prefixes it with the surfaced marker from the
detached child:

```
warn: previous finalize failure (<timestamp_ns>-<pid>):
  timestamp_ns=...
  pid=...
  repo=/tmp/vc-x1-guard-...
  bookmark=
  error=refusing squash @ → @-: ...
```

## Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers

input.new_string:
Output captured from a real run (`/tmp/vc-x1-guard-oRTPcT` is the
fixture). Step 1 is refused synchronously with exit 1:

```
$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0
error: refusing squash @ → @-: the source message has ochid: trailers the destination's message lacks (/abc123abc123) — --use-destination-message would drop them; merge the messages by hand (`jj describe @- -R /tmp/vc-x1-guard-oRTPcT`) or clear the source's description, then retry
```

Step 2 with the description cleared squashes normally, exit 0:

```
$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0
finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-guard-oRTPcT
finalize: squashing @ → @-...
finalize: done
```

Step 3's parent passes pre-flight on the still-clean `@` and
detaches; the trailer-bearing describe then lands inside the
child's 5-second delay window:

```
$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 5 --detach
finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-guard-oRTPcT
finalize: detached (pid 144021)
```

The retry after the child exits surfaces the child's failure

content.text:
Done. The section now shows verbatim transcripts from a single real run, in the same `$ command` + output style as the push+finalize section:

- **Step 1** — the synchronous refusal with the full error text (at-risk trailer `/abc123abc123`, the `jj describe` recovery hint, real fixture path).
- **Step 2** — the normal squash: `squash @ → @- ... finalize: done`.
- **Step 3** — the detach line (`finalize: detached (pid 144021)`), then the retry output where the `warn: previous finalize failure` marker block appears first — its pid matches the detach line since both came from the same run — followed by the synchronous refusal again.

Everything in the output blocks is unedited capture, so a user running the recipe will see exactly this shape (with their own paths/pids). Ready to commit — please review.

message.content: The "real run" looks good but it points out a couple problems the "error: ..." message is long and a single line, can we wrap or ..?. In the last example we see "error= ..." and "error: ..." that's redundant maybe remove the "error=..."? Thoughts.

content.text:
Good catches, both. Here's my read:

**1. The long single-line error.** The message comes from `check_squash_keeps_ochids` as one `format!` string, so the fix is to restructure it in code, not in the docs. I'd break it into short lines, putting the at-risk trailers on their own line so multiple trailers list cleanly:

```
error: refusing squash @ → @-: the squash would drop ochid: trailers
the destination's message lacks:
  /abc123abc123
merge the messages by hand (`jj describe @- -R <repo>`) or clear the
source's description, then retry
```

One wrinkle: the failure marker writes `error={err}` as a `key=value` line, so a multi-line error would break the marker's one-line-per-key shape. I'd flatten newlines to spaces when writing the marker — terminal output gets the readable wrap, the marker file stays parseable.

**2. The `error=` / `error:` redundancy.** The duplication is real but each line is doing a different job: `error=` is the marker's payload (the only record of *why the child failed* — dropping it would gut the marker), while `error:` is the retry failing *live* against the same still-bad state. So I wouldn't remove `error=` from the marker. Instead I'd fix it in the recipe: make step 3's retry the *resolution* — clear the description first, then retry. The transcript then shows the surfaced marker (with its `error=` explaining what happened) followed by `finalize: done` — the error text appears once, no duplicate, and the recipe teaches the recovery path instead of hitting the same wall twice.

Both changes are small: reword one `format!` + one-line flatten in `write_failure_marker`, re-capture the transcripts, and adjust the step-3 recipe. The unit tests don't touch the message text, so they're unaffected.

Want me to do both as part of `-1`?

lastPrompt: The "real run" looks good but it points out a couple problems the "error: ..." message is long and a single line, can we wrap or ..?. In the last example we see "error= ..." and "error: ..." that's re…

message.content: yes, do both. But why does the last one have the "warn: ..." output seems unnecessary and we don't see it in the others?

content.text:
The "warn:" block is there *because* step 3's failure happened in a detached background child. In steps 1–2 finalize runs inline, so the error prints straight to your terminal at exit — nothing else needed. In step 3 the parent already returned (preflight passed; the bad describe landed later), so the child's refusal had no terminal to land on — the marker + "warn:" surfacing on the next `vc-x1` run is the only way that background failure ever becomes visible. It's the marker plumbing doing its job, not noise; I'll make the README say that explicitly.

Now both changes. First the error-message rewrap and marker flattening in `src/finalize.rs`:

content.text:
Both changes are in, with fresh transcripts captured after reinstalling:

**Code (`src/finalize.rs`):**
- The refusal message is now multi-line: headline, the at-risk trailers each on their own indented line (so multiple trailers list cleanly), then the two-line recovery hint. Longest fixed line is well under 80; only the line embedding the repo path can run long.
- `write_failure_marker` flattens the error to one line (`split_whitespace` + join), keeping the marker's one-`key=value`-per-line shape; its doc comment notes why.
- fmt/clippy/tests green (the unit tests don't assert message text, so no churn), binary reinstalled before capture.

**README:**
- All transcripts re-captured from a single real run with the new format — step 1 now shows the wrapped error.
- Step 3's retry is now the *resolution*: clear the description, retry → the transcript shows the `warn:` marker block once, then `finalize: done`, exit 0. The duplicate `error:` is gone, and the recipe teaches the recovery path.
- Added the explanation you asked about: the `warn:` block appears only in step 3 because that refusal happened in a detached child after the parent returned — no terminal to land on — so the marker surfaced on the next run is the only way the failure becomes visible; steps 1–2 fail inline at the prompt and need no marker.

Ready to commit — please review.

message.content:
In the last one example which uses `write_failure_marker()` there is the warning showing the "error=refusing squash .." but the output ends with:
finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-guard-EgDD76
finalize: squashing @ → @-...
finalize: done

So we had a "warning" with an error that says refusing to squash but the output implies it was squashed, confused.

content.text:
Your confusion is exactly the right reading of what's on screen — and it points at a real UX gap. What actually happened is two events from two different runs streaming together with no boundary:

1. **Earlier run (the detached child):** it refused the squash and — having no terminal — recorded that refusal in the marker. The `error=refusing squash …` line is that *historical* record being replayed.
2. **Current run (the retry):** by then the description was cleared, so the live squash legitimately proceeded — `finalize: done`.

So the output is truthful but reads as self-contradictory: nothing visually separates "here's what failed *last time*" from "here's what's happening *now*". The `warn: previous finalize failure` header tries to say it, but the trailing `error=` line sits right above the current run's output and wins the reader's attention.

I'd fix it in the tool, not just the docs — anyone hitting this for real will have the same confusion. Proposal for `surface_previous_failures`:

- Slightly sharpen the header: `warn: previous detached finalize failed (1781…-185406):`
- After all markers are printed, emit one closing line that draws the boundary:
  `warn: failures above are from earlier detached runs (markers now removed) — current command output follows`

The transcript would then read:

```
warn: previous detached finalize failed (1781196351549804588-185406):
  timestamp_ns=...
  ...
  error=refusing squash @ → @-: ...
warn: failures above are from earlier detached runs (markers now
removed) — current command output follows
finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-guard-EgDD76
finalize: squashing @ → @-...
finalize: done
```

…and the README prose gets one sentence making the two-runs timeline explicit. Re-capture the transcripts after, as before. Want me to apply that?

Reordering the surfacing to after the current run is also doable and arguably better — last thing on screen sticks. Let me check `main.rs`'s dispatch structure to find all exit paths:

demonstrations — they share one scratch repo only for convenience;

```bash

jj git init "$repo"

jj commit -m 'prev journal' -R "$repo"

# an ochid trailer; finalize exits 1 naming the trailer

jj describe -R "$repo" -m 'new journal

ochid: /abc123abc123'

# proceeds

vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0

The guard fires before any push, so no remote is needed; a plain
scratch repo suffices. The three examples below are independent
demonstrations — they share one scratch repo only for convenience;
the final retry is the resolution of example 3:

```bash
repo=$(mktemp -u /tmp/vc-x1-guard-XXXXXX)
jj git init "$repo"

# previous journal: committed, no trailer
echo a > "$repo/file.txt"
jj commit -m 'prev journal' -R "$repo"

# example 1 — synchronous refusal: journal described on @ WITH
# an ochid trailer; finalize exits 1 naming the trailer
echo b >> "$repo/file.txt"
jj describe -R "$repo" -m 'new journal

ochid: /abc123abc123'
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0

# example 2 — normal case: clear the description; the squash
# proceeds
jj describe -R "$repo" -m ''
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0

# example 3 — detached race: a clean @ passes the parent's
# pre-flight, then a describe lands inside the delay window —
# the child's re-check refuses and drops a failure marker
echo c >> "$repo/file.txt"
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 5 --detach
jj describe -R "$repo" -m 'late journal

ochid: /def456def456'

# resolution of example 3: after the child exits (~5s), clear the
# description and retry; the current run's output comes first,
# then the child's failure marker is surfaced as a loud
# historical block (any vc-x1 command surfaces pending markers)
sleep 6
jj describe -R "$repo" -m ''
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0

# cleanup
rm -rf "$repo"
```

fixture). Example 1 is refused synchronously with exit 1:

the destination's message lacks:

the source's description, then retry
```

Example 2 with the description cleared squashes normally, exit 0:

finalize: squash @ → @- in /tmp/vc-x1-guard-GVHMGK
finalize: squashing @ → @-...
finalize: done
```

Example 3's parent passes pre-flight on the still-clean `@` and
detaches; the trailer-bearing describe then lands inside the
child's 5-second delay window:

finalize: detached (pid 256261)
```

Unlike examples 1–2 the refusal happens in the background — the
parent already returned, so there is no terminal for the child's
error to land on. The failure marker is what makes it visible:
the next `vc-x1` run prints it *after its own output*, under a

Done, all three asks:

**Surfacing reordered (code):** `main.rs` now runs the command first and surfaces markers after — the dispatch result is captured, `surface_previous_failures()` runs on every exit path of the dispatch (success or failure), and the exec-child skip is preserved via a flag computed up front. `surface_previous_failures` opens with a loud banner — `warn: ==== failure(s) from previous detached finalize run(s) ====` — and only prints anything when markers exist. The per-marker header now says "previous **detached** finalize failed".

**The confusing transcript is fixed.** The retry now reads in honest chronological-looking order:

```
finalize: done
warn: ==== failure(s) from previous detached finalize run(s) ====
warn: previous detached finalize failed (1781213067506771156-256261):
  ...
```

Current run completes, then the historical block — no more "refused but squashed?" whiplash.

**Steps → Examples (README):** you're right that they're independent demonstrations, not a sequence — the section now says so explicitly ("share one scratch repo only for convenience; the final retry is the resolution of example 3"), the recipe comments are relabeled `example 1/2/3` + `resolution of example 3`, and all transcripts are fresh captures from one real run (`/tmp/vc-x1-guard-GVHMGK`, pid 256261 consistent across the detach line and the marker).

Cargo cycle green (fmt, clippy `-D warnings`, 391 tests), binary reinstalled. One scope note for the record: the surfacing reorder changes output ordering for *all* `vc-x1` commands, not just finalize — I've added it to the `-1` ladder rung. Ready to commit — please review.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# gen-exmpl-1-3.sh — regenerate the example transcripts for
# README.md's "Testing the ochid-trailer guard" section.
#
# - Scaffolds a scratch jj repo under /tmp (offline; no remote).
# - Runs example 1 (synchronous refusal), example 2 (normal
#   squash), example 3 (detached race -> failure marker), and the
#   example-3 resolution (retry succeeds, then surfaces the
#   marker after the current run's output).
# - Prints each `$ vc-x1 ...` command followed by its actual
#   output, separated by `==== ... ====` banners, ready to paste
#   into the README's code blocks.
# - Refuses to start if failure markers are already pending — the
#   resolution step would surface (and delete) them.
# - Removes the scratch repo on exit.

set -eu

status_glob="$HOME/.cache/vc-x1/finalize-status/*.status"

if compgen -G "$status_glob" >/dev/null; then
    echo "error: pending finalize failure markers exist — run any" >&2
    echo "vc-x1 command to surface them, then re-run this script" >&2
    exit 1
fi

repo=$(mktemp -u /tmp/vc-x1-guard-XXXXXX)
trap 'rm -rf "$repo"' EXIT

hr() { printf '\n==== %s ====\n\n' "$1"; }

hr "scaffold: $repo"
jj git init "$repo"
echo a > "$repo/file.txt"
jj commit -m 'prev journal' -R "$repo"

hr "example 1 — synchronous refusal (expect exit 1)"
echo b >> "$repo/file.txt"
jj describe -R "$repo" -m 'new journal

ochid: /abc123abc123'
printf '$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "%s" --squash --delay 0\n' "$repo"
rc=0
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0 || rc=$?
echo "exit=$rc"

hr "example 2 — cleared description squashes normally (expect exit 0)"
jj describe -R "$repo" -m ''
printf '$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "%s" --squash --delay 0\n' "$repo"
rc=0
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0 || rc=$?
echo "exit=$rc"

hr "example 3 — detached race (child refuses, drops a marker)"
echo c >> "$repo/file.txt"
printf '$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "%s" --squash --delay 5 --detach\n' "$repo"
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 5 --detach
jj describe -R "$repo" -m 'late journal

ochid: /def456def456'

# Wait for the child's failure marker (up to 15s).
found=
for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
    if compgen -G "$status_glob" >/dev/null; then
        found=1
        break
    fi
    sleep 0.5
done
[ -n "$found" ] || { echo "error: no failure marker appeared" >&2; exit 1; }

hr "resolution of example 3 — retry succeeds, then surfaces the marker"
jj describe -R "$repo" -m ''
printf '$ vc-x1 finalize --repo "%s" --squash --delay 0\n' "$repo"
rc=0
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$repo" --squash --delay 0 || rc=$?
echo "exit=$rc"

hr "done (scratch repo removed on exit)"

input.content:
# Support scripts

Helper scripts for maintaining this repo's docs and examples.

## Gen Example 1 - 3 output

[`gen-exmpl-1-3.sh`](gen-exmpl-1-3.sh) regenerates the transcripts
for the
[Testing the ochid-trailer guard](../README.md#testing-the-ochid-trailer-guard)
section of the top-level README:

- Scaffolds a scratch jj repo under `/tmp` (offline; no remote
  needed).
- Runs example 1 (synchronous refusal), example 2 (normal squash),
  example 3 (detached race → failure marker), and the example-3
  resolution (retry succeeds, then surfaces the marker after the
  current run's output).
- Prints each `$ vc-x1 …` command followed by its actual output,
  separated by `==== … ====` banners, ready to paste into the
  README's code blocks.
- Refuses to start if failure markers are already pending — the
  resolution step would surface (and delete) them.
- Removes the scratch repo on exit.

Run it from the project root (or anywhere — it only touches
`/tmp` and the marker directory):

```bash
support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh
```

Note: in a terminal, example 3's detached child inherits
`/dev/tty`, so its refusal may also print ~5 s after the detach
line. The marker block in the resolution output is the durable
record of that same failure.

- Refuses to start if failure markers are already pending — the
  resolution step would surface (and delete) them.
- Removes the scratch repo on exit.

Run it from the project root (or anywhere — it only touches
`/tmp` and the marker directory):

```bash
support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh
```

Note: in a terminal, example 3's detached child inherits
`/dev/tty`, so its refusal may also print ~5 s after the detach
line. The marker block in the resolution output is the durable
record of that same failure.

content.text:
All in place — the script ran end-to-end on the first try and jj tracks both new files:

- **`support/gen-exmpl-1-3.sh`** (executable) — scaffolds the scratch repo, runs examples 1–3 plus the resolution, and prints `$ command` + actual output under `==== … ====` banners ready to paste into the README. Two defensive touches: it refuses to start if failure markers are already pending (the resolution step would surface and delete real ones), and a `trap` removes the scratch repo on any exit. The verification run produced output matching the README transcripts exactly (modulo paths/pids).
- **`support/README.md`** — `## Gen Example 1 - 3 output` documenting the script: what it runs, the paste-ready output format, the marker guard, and a note that in a terminal the detached child's refusal may also print mid-run via `/dev/tty` (the marker block is the durable record).
- **`/README.md`** — new `## Support` section (after `## Testing`) pointing at `support/`, with `### gen-exmpl-1-3.sh` giving the one-sentence summary and a link to the `support/README.md` section. Also added a "regenerate these transcripts with…" cross-link inside the guard section itself, so the next person refreshing the transcripts finds the script.

No Rust changes this round, so the earlier cargo cycle still stands. Ready to commit — please review.
